title: penstemon menziesii f. douglasii
intro: Penstemon menziesii f. douglasii, also known as the Douglas' beardtongue, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae), native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The Douglas' beardtongue has been shown to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues and reducing fever, making it a valuable plant in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of Penstemon menziesii f. douglasii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds.
